title: Summarize a passage in 3 bullets
response:
- Before the 1950s, cargo was shipped via break-bulk shipping — sacks, barrels, and crates loaded piece by piece — so loading a medium-sized ship could take a week and cost around $5.86 per ton.
- In 1956, trucking entrepreneur Malcom McLean sent the converted tanker Ideal X from Newark to Houston carrying 58 uniform metal boxes that could move straight from truck to ship, cutting loading costs to roughly 16 cents per ton.
- The real breakthrough came when international standards agreed by 1968 settled common container dimensions and twistlock corner castings, driving shipping costs so low that transport largely stopped mattering to where goods were made, reshaping global trade and enabling far-flung supply chains.
scoreDetail: fidelity: 9/10, coverage: 9/10, format compliance: 9/10, concision: 6/10 — Facts are accurate and all three key ideas are covered in exactly three one-sentence bullets, but the third bullet is a run-on stuffed with too many clauses.
